articles.title: Tech Sector Flashes Most Negative Signal Since January Ahead Of Nvidia Earnings
articles.excerpt: Technology sector momentum gauges have turned negative for the first time since January 16th, led by weakness in smaller-cap stocks. Market breadth is historically narrow, with mega-caps driving index highs while a majority of stocks have moved below their 200-day moving averages

articles.title: US Says China to Buy $17 Billion in Farm Goods Annually
articles.excerpt: China has agreed to buy at least $17 billion of agricultural products annually through 2028, the US said after Donald Trump and Xi Jinping held a major summit in Beijing last week. Bloomberg's Stephen Engle has the details.

articles.title: This chart shows why AI will eventually mean lower bond yields
articles.excerpt: Potential labor-market weakness following the widespread adoption of AI practices in the workplace should eventually lead to lower interest rates, Dario Perkins contends.

articles.title: A Summer Struggle To Be Followed By A Resumption Of The Bull Market
articles.excerpt: The S&P 500 has surged 16% in six weeks, driven by robust 27.7% Q1 earnings growth and raised analyst estimates. I am positioning more defensively short-term, citing rising interest rates, deteriorating market breadth, and persistent inflation as headwinds for risk assets.

articles.title: S&P500 Forecast: Rising Yields Threaten Wall Street Rally This Week
articles.excerpt: S&P 500 futures fall as rising Treasury yields, oil prices and Nvidia earnings pressure Wall Street ahead of a critical week for US stocks.

articles.title: 'Higher For Longer' Is Making A Comeback
articles.excerpt: With inflation fears resurfacing and bond markets under pressure, what will monetary policy from the Federal Reserve look like through the end of the year?

articles.title: U.K. Gilt Yields Climb to Multiyear Highs on Inflation, Political Concerns
articles.excerpt: Yields on 30-year gilts hit a high last seen in 1998, while 10-year yields were at their highest since 2008.

articles.title: The Major Differences Between This Bull Stock Market And The Late 1990s

articles.title: Here's where Treasury yields will peak and open up a rare opportunity to buy stocks and bonds, says Wall Street veteran
articles.excerpt: The Treasury yield could peak near 5% in coming weeks, which will be a chance to buy stocks, says Ed Yardeni.

articles.title: IKEA franchiser slashes 850 jobs in cost-cutting drive as consumer spending falls
articles.excerpt: Inter IKEA, which franchises the Swedish budget home furniture brand in 63 ‌countries, is laying off 850 workers in a cost-cutting drive as consumer demand falls and the blue big-box retailer works to become more efficient and bring down prices.

articles.title: Britain's prospective next PM tries to placate bond markets after sell-off, gilts steady
articles.excerpt: U.K. gilts remain in focus on Monday after last week's rout. Bond markets are trying to gauge whether the U.K.'s would-be prime minister, Andy Burnham, could throw away the fiscal rule book.

articles.title: Treasury yields rise amid global bond rout as inflation fears grip investors
articles.excerpt: Global bond yields rose on Monday as fears of resurgent inflation grip financial markets.

articles.title: Cerebras's Reversal, Friday Stocks Retreat On Inflation, Stocks May Head Lower
articles.excerpt: Cerebras debuted with extreme hype, surging to a $100B+ valuation on just two paying UAE customers, then swiftly dropped nearly 28%. CBRS trades at 134x trailing revenue—over five times Nvidia's multiple—despite heavy customer concentration and unproven large-scale production.

articles.title: Gina Rinehart's Hancock Prospecting adds defence stocks to US portfolio
articles.excerpt: Hancock Prospecting, owned by Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart, has added ​defence, gold and rare-earths holdings ‌to its $3.3 billion U.S. portfolio this year, filings showed on Monday.
